name: Leptos
description: A full-stack Rust framework leveraging fine-grained reactivity to build fast, reliable web applications

description.value: A full-stack Rust framework leveraging fine-grained reactivity to build fast, reliable web applications

active-maintenance.comment: Very active development with 5,138 commits, 356 contributors, regular releases

reactivity-model.comment: Fine-grained reactivity with signals; when a signal changes, only that specific DOM node updates without Virtual DOM diffing

component-model.comment: Components defined as Rust functions with #[component] attribute macro returning impl IntoView

rendering-paradigm.comment: Declarative JSX-like view macro syntax with fine-grained reactive updates

state-management-approach.comment: Built-in reactive primitives with signals, effects, and resources; context API for global state

opinionatedness.comment: Provides full-stack capabilities with recommended patterns but allows flexibility in architecture

backward-compatibility.comment: Still pre-1.0 (v0.8.x); APIs have stabilized but breaking changes occur between minor versions

compiles-to.comment: Compiles Rust to WebAssembly for the browser; also compiles to native for server-side rendering

typescript-support.comment: Rust framework; not applicable - uses Rust's type system instead

template-syntax.comment: view! macro with RSX (JSX-like) syntax; also supports builder pattern

styling-approach.comment: No built-in styling; works with Tailwind, CSS modules via community crates like stylers and stylance

build-required.comment: Requires Rust toolchain with wasm32-unknown-unknown target; cargo-leptos handles build process

wasm-interop.comment: Uses wasm-bindgen and web-sys for JavaScript interop; direct DOM access through web APIs

reactive-primitives.comment: signal() creates reactive state; RwSignal for read-write access

computed-values.comment: Memo for derived/computed values that auto-update and cache results

two-way-binding.comment: bind:value directive for two-way binding on form elements

event-handling.comment: on:click, on:input and other event attributes in view macro

conditional-rendering.comment: <Show> component for conditional rendering with fallback

list-rendering.comment: <For> component for efficient keyed list rendering

component-slots.comment: children prop for content projection; named slots via props

teleport-portal.comment: <Portal> component for rendering outside component tree

suspense.comment: <Suspense> and <Transition> components for async loading boundaries with fallback UI

error-boundaries.comment: <ErrorBoundary> component for catching and displaying errors in component tree

context-provide-inject.comment: provide_context and use_context for dependency injection without prop drilling

refs-direct-dom-access.comment: NodeRef for direct DOM element access; integrates with web-sys

official-router.comment: leptos_router is the official first-party routing solution, included in main repo

nested-routes.comment: Full support for nested routes via <ParentRoute> and <Outlet>

dynamic-routes.comment: Parameterized routes with use_params and use_params_map hooks

route-guards.comment: Navigation guards via before_leave and redirect mechanisms

lazy-loading-routes.comment: #[lazy_route] macro and LazyRoute trait for WASM binary splitting

scroll-restoration.comment: Built-in scroll restoration support in router

built-in-state.comment: signal() for local reactive state, derived signals for computed state

built-in-global-state.comment: Context API with provide_context/use_context for app-wide state

global-state-library.comment: Built-in context and signals sufficient for most cases; leptos-use provides additional utilities

devtools-state-inspection.comment: No official browser devtools extension; debugging via Rust tooling and console logging

state-persistence.comment: No built-in persistence layer; available via community crates

time-travel-debugging.comment: Not supported; fine-grained reactivity makes traditional time-travel less applicable

official-cli.comment: cargo-leptos is the official build tool with project scaffolding via cargo-generate templates

hot-module-replacement.comment: cargo-leptos provides hot reloading with CSS hot-reload (no page reload for CSS changes)

browser-devtools.comment: No official browser devtools extension; uses standard browser WASM debugging

vs-code-extension.comment: vscode-leptos extension for workspace configuration; leptos-fmt for RSX formatting; rust-analyzer integration

error-messages.comment: Rust's excellent compiler errors plus Leptos-specific diagnostics; improved in v0.5.3+ with rust-analyzer support

documentation-quality.comment: Comprehensive Leptos Book with tutorials, API docs on docs.rs, video tutorials, CodeSandbox playground

playground.comment: CodeSandbox examples available but no official REPL playground due to Rust compilation requirements

bundle-size-core.comment: ~150 kB WASM gzipped for minimal app; varies significantly by features used. WASM parses faster than equivalent JS.

bundle-size-full.comment: ~300 kB WASM gzipped with router and common features; islands architecture can reduce to ~145 kB. Not directly comparable to JS bundle sizes.

virtual-dom.comment: No Virtual DOM; fine-grained reactivity updates specific DOM nodes directly

compile-time-optimization.comment: Rust compiler performs extensive optimizations; view! macro generates efficient code at compile time

tree-shaking.comment: Rust's dead code elimination; wasm-opt further optimizes WASM binaries

code-splitting.comment: WASM binary splitting via #[lazy] macro and cargo leptos --split

ssr-capable.comment: Full SSR support with hydration; works with Actix-web and Axum

streaming-ssr.comment: HTTP streaming SSR built-in; out-of-order streaming with Suspense boundaries

partial-hydration.comment: Islands architecture supported; ~80% WASM size reduction for partial hydration scenarios

js-framework-benchmark.comment: Estimated ~1.08 geometric mean; performs near top of frameworks, beating Vue, React, Angular. Exact score varies by version.

npm-weekly-downloads.comment: Rust crate, not npm package; distributed via crates.io

component-libraries.comment: Thaw, Leptix, Leptos Material, Rust Radix, Rust shadcn/ui, Leptonic

meta-framework.comment: Leptos itself is full-stack; no separate meta-framework needed. Works with Actix-web or Axum for server.

major-companies-using.comment: Growing adoption in Rust community; commercial users reported but specific company names not publicly listed

official-sponsor-backer.comment: Community-driven project; no major corporate backing

discord-community-size.comment: ~5,200 members in Leptos Discord as of December 2025

stack-overflow-questions.comment: Limited Stack Overflow presence; community primarily uses Discord and GitHub Discussions

job-market-demand.comment: Niche; primarily adopted by Rust enthusiasts and teams already using Rust backends

official-testing-library.comment: No official testing library; leptos_test community crate available based on dom_testing_library

testing-library-name.comment: Recommended approach: wasm-bindgen-test for unit tests, Playwright for E2E

component-testing.comment: Via wasm-pack test with wasm-bindgen-test; mount components to DOM and verify

e2e-testing-integration.comment: cargo-leptos has built-in Playwright integration via end2end-cmd config

snapshot-testing.comment: Supported via outerHTML comparison in wasm-bindgen-test

mobile-framework.comment: Works with Tauri 2.0 for mobile (iOS/Android); Leptos is an official Tauri frontend template

pwa-support.comment: Can be configured as PWA; no built-in tooling but compatible with service worker patterns

desktop-framework.comment: Official integration with Tauri for desktop applications (Windows, macOS, Linux)

learning-curve.comment: Steep learning curve; requires Rust knowledge plus framework concepts (signals, async, macros)

concepts-to-learn.comment: Rust ownership, signals, effects, resources, suspense, SSR modes, view! macro, server functions, routing, context

time-to-hello-world.comment: ~10 minutes with Rust toolchain installed; includes cargo-leptos install and template generation

migration-path.comment: No migration guides from JS frameworks; fundamentally different paradigm (Rust vs JavaScript)

incremental-adoption.comment: Cannot be incrementally added to existing JS applications; requires full Rust toolchain
